generatedSentence: A solitary oarsman, Kael, propelled his vessel through the murky canals of a forgotten locale, a relic from a past chapter. A strange dewdrop clung to the chrome-plated hull, a testament to ancient alchemy. He was about to begin a new chapter, seeking a vacation from the oppressive institutions that controlled the market. His inventory was sparse: a single kiwi fruit and a peculiar vegetable, but his spirit was unbound. Whispers of a new option for freedom had reached him, hidden in summaries passed near rusting tankers, promising an opening away from this warzone. The air, thick with the scent of napalm and something damaging, always felt reactive here, hinting at the peril he was about to face. Kael's journey was immediately challenged by a hostile faceoff with a notorious warlock, known for his damaging sorcery and the knack for turning people into turkeys. This abomination had sabotaged the navigation systems, making the usually calm waters a treacherous raceway. The loppy, fragmented machinery of the old city began to dissolve into a shimmering, electronic chaos, as the warlock applied a potent whammy. Kael felt a sudden loss of control, needing to respond quickly. The warlock’s language, a series of naughtiest growls and threats, promised an overdose of misery. This encounter was more than a mere opening; it was a brutal kickboxing match of wills, with the warlock's relentless power making Kael's situation squiffy and dire. The conflict escalated into a full-blown battle, Kael now embodying a berserker spirit, his actions reminiscent of ancestral athletics. He knew this was a vital chapter in his vendetta. He dodged a rain of glittering pearl projectiles, each one capable of causing an arithmetic-like precision of pain. The warlock's minions, a horde of munchkins, attacked from all sides, their sing-song vocals eliciting a primal response. Kael, with unbound resolve, tackled one, sending another flying from a trampoline-like arch, his movements a symphony of kinetic energy. He realized this fight, with its variations of attacks, was an ultra challenge, a true test of his kin and their shared dreamcatchers legacy. In a climactic move, Kael delivered a decisive whammy, his pearl-handled blade finding its mark, causing the abomination warlock's power to dissolve. The battle ended not with a kiss of victory, but a quiet victory. Kael felt healed, though still needing a vet's attention, perhaps even a trip to a hospital restaurant, for his strained jaw. He carried no vices, only the memory of the relentless fight. With the warzone now pacified, he could finally apply himself to a peaceful life, perhaps even enjoy a simple pumpkin and spinach gnocchi. The memory of the fight, like a dewdrop, would eventually evaporate, leaving only the lessons learned. His ultimate option, a serene existence, was finally within reach.

The loppy, fragmented machinery of the old city began to dissolve into a shimmering, electronic chaos, as the warlock applied a potent whammy. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined figure with a chrome cybernetic arm, wearing a dark, form-fitting suit adorned with glowing teal accents and a high-tech tactical visor. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Kael, the oarsman, navigates his sleek, low-profile skiff through a bioluminescent, murky canal, a towering, derelict cyberpunk city in the background, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A menacing warlock, surrounded by crackling violet energy, unleashes a torrent of arcane lightning at Kael's skiff, taken from a dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Kael, now a berserker, deftly dodges a swarm of glittering, pearl-like projectiles, his chrome arm glowing, close-up, action blur. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Kael stands victorious over the defeated warlock's dissolving form, holding a pearl-handled blade, a calm expression on his face, wide shot.

definitions.definition: A man who rows a boat, either alone or with others.
(authorship) One of the main sections into which the text of a book is divided.
definitions.example: Detective novel writers try to keep up the suspense until the last chapter.

definitions.definition: A section of a social or religious body.
A sequence (of events), especially when presumed related and likely to continue.
A decretal epistle.
A location or compartment.
To divide into chapters.
To put into a chapter.
(with "out") To use administrative procedure to remove someone.
To take to task.
A droplet of water formed as dew.
(1800s) A slow pitch.
To impair the soundness, goodness, or value of; to harm or cause destruction.
definitions.example: Be careful not to damage any of the fragile items while unpacking them.

definitions.definition: To undergo damage.
An act of causing damage.
Harmful; injurious; causing damage.
definitions.example: The politician resigned after damaging information was revealed.

definitions.definition: A form of film punctuation in which there is a gradual transition from one scene to the next
To terminate a union of multiple members actively, as by disbanding.
definitions.example: The ruling party or coalition sometimes dissolves parliament early when the polls are favorable, hoping to reconvene with a larger majority.

definitions.definition: To destroy, make disappear.
To liquify, melt into a fluid.
To be melted, changed into a fluid.
To disintegrate chemically into a solution by immersion into a liquid or gas.
To be disintegrated by such immersion.
To disperse, drive apart a group of persons.
To break the continuity of; to disconnect; to loosen; to undo; to separate.
To annul; to rescind; to discharge or release.
definitions.example: to dissolve an injunction

definitions.definition: To shift from one shot to another by having the former fade out as the latter fades in.
To resolve itself as by dissolution.
To solve; to clear up; to resolve.
To relax by pleasure; to make powerless.
A highly flammable, viscous substance, designed to stick to the body while burning, used in warfare as an incendiary especially in wooded areas.
To spray or attack with this substance.
The shell that surrounds the kernel of a nut.
A short book summarizing an area of law.
To summarize (from the term in a nutshell).
To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
To (cause to) touch lightly or slightly; to come into contact.
definitions.example: His ball kissed the black into the corner pocket.

definitions.definition: Of two or more people, to touch each other's lips together, usually to express love or affection or passion.
To treat with fondness.
That reacts or responds to a stimulus
That readily takes part in reactions
definitions.example: francium is the most reactive of the alkali metals.

definitions.definition: Characterized by induction or capacitance rather than resistance.
Reacting to the past rather than anticipating the future, not predictive.
Moving around; astir.
definitions.example: After my bout with Guillan-Barre Syndrome, it took me 6 months to be up and about again.

definitions.definition: In existence; being in evidence; apparent.
definitions.example: This idea has been about for a while but has only recently become fashionable.

definitions.definition: Near; in the vicinity or neighbourhood.
definitions.example: I had my keys just a minute ago, so they must be about somewhere.

definitions.definition: On all sides; around.
definitions.example: I looked about at the scenery that surrounded me.

definitions.definition: Here and there; around; in one place and another; up and down.
definitions.example: Bits of old machinery were lying about.

definitions.definition: From one place or position to another in succession; indicating repeated movement or activity.
definitions.example: walking about;  rushing about;  jumping about;  thrashing about

definitions.definition: Indicating unproductive or unstructured activity.
definitions.example: messing about;   fooling about;  loafing about

definitions.definition: Nearly; approximately; with close correspondence in quality, manner, degree, quantity, or time; almost.
definitions.example: Dinner's about ready.

definitions.definition: Near; in the vicinity.
To a reversed order; half round; facing in the opposite direction; from a contrary point of view.
definitions.example: to face about;  to turn oneself about

definitions.definition: In succession; one after another; in the course of events.
In circuit; circularly; by a circuitous way; around the outside; in circumference.
definitions.example: a mile about, and a third of a mile across

definitions.definition: In a circle around; all round; on every side of; on the outside of.
definitions.example: The snake was coiled about his ankle.

definitions.definition: Over or upon different parts of; through or over in various directions; here and there in; to and fro in; throughout.
definitions.example: He was well known about town.

definitions.definition: Indicates that something will happen very soon; indicates a plan or intention to do something.
Concerning; with regard to; on account of; on the subject of; to affect.
definitions.example: He talked a lot about his childhood.

definitions.definition: Concerned with; engaged in; intent on.
definitions.example: to be about one's business

definitions.definition: Within or in the immediate neighborhood of; in contiguity or proximity to; near, as to place.
definitions.example: I can't find my reading glasses, but they must be somewhere about the house.

definitions.definition: On one's person; nearby the person.
definitions.example: I had no weapon about me but a stick.

definitions.definition: In or near, as in mental faculties or in the possession of; under the control of; at one's command; in one's makeup.
definitions.example: He has his wits about him.

definitions.definition: An abominable act; a disgusting vice; a despicable habit.
The feeling of extreme disgust and hatred
A state that excites detestation or abhorrence; pollution.
That which is abominable, shamefully vile; an object that excites disgust and hatred; very often with religious undertones.
A male magic-user; a male witch.
The guinea fowl (family Numididae).
A bird in the genus Meleagris with a fan-shaped tail and wattled neck, especially the wild turkey (Meleagris gallopavo, now domesticated).
The flesh or meat of this bird eaten as food.
definitions.example: All week after Thanksgiving, I had turkey sandwiches for lunch.

definitions.definition: With a distinguishing word: a bird resembling the Meleagris gallopavo (for example, the bush turkey or brush turkey (Alectura lathami), and the water turkey (Anhinga anhinga)).
An act of throwing three strikes in a row.
A patient feigning symptoms; a person faking illness or injury; a malingerer.
A pack carried by a lumberman; a bindle; also, a large travel bag, a suitcase.
A failure.
definitions.example: That film was a turkey.

definitions.definition: (usually mildly) A foolish or inept person.
definitions.example: The turkey cut in front of me and then berated me for running into him.

definitions.definition: Alternative form of teepee
A tank ship, a vessel used to transport large quantities of liquid.
A tank truck.
A fuel tanker, petrol tanker, road tanker.
A tank car
Member of a tank crew, or of an armoured unit.
A longboard.
definitions.example: I swung the tanker around just in time to take off with the lip

definitions.definition: A body of words, and set of methods of combining them (called a grammar), understood by a community and used as a form of communication.
definitions.example: Deaf and mute people communicate using languages like ASL.

definitions.definition: The ability to communicate using words.
definitions.example: the gift of language

definitions.definition: A sublanguage: the slang of a particular community or jargon of a particular specialist field.
definitions.example: legal language;   the language of chemistry

definitions.definition: The expression of thought (the communication of meaning) in a specified way; that which communicates something, as language does.
definitions.example: body language;   the language of the eyes

definitions.definition: A body of sounds, signs and/or signals by which animals communicate, and by which plants are sometimes also thought to communicate.
A computer language; a machine language.
Manner of expression.
The particular words used in a speech or a passage of text.
definitions.example: The language he used to talk to me was obscene.

definitions.definition: Profanity.
To communicate by language; to express in language.
The method used to begin play in ice hockey.
To circle or revolve around another object.
definitions.example: The Earth orbits the Sun.

definitions.definition: To move around the general vicinity of something.
definitions.example: The harried mother had a cloud of children orbiting her, begging for sweets.

definitions.definition: To place an object into an orbit around a planet.
definitions.example: A rocket was used to orbit the satellite.

definitions.definition: One of a set of choices that can be made.
The freedom or right to choose.

A contract giving the holder the right to buy or sell an asset at a set strike price; can apply to financial market transactions, or to ordinary transactions for tangible assets such as a residence or automobile.
To purchase an option on something.
definitions.example: The new novel was optioned by the film studio, but they'll probably never decide to make a movie from it.

definitions.definition: To configure, by setting an option.
To make something accessible or allow for passage by moving from a shut position.
definitions.example: Turn the doorknob to open the door.

definitions.definition: To make (an open space, etc.) by clearing away an obstacle or obstacles, in order to allow for passage, access, or visibility.
definitions.example: He opened a path through the undergrowth.

definitions.definition: To bring up, broach.
definitions.example: I don't want to open that subject.

definitions.definition: To enter upon, begin.
definitions.example: to open a case in court, or a meeting

definitions.definition: To spread; to expand into an open or loose position.
definitions.example: to open a closed fist

definitions.definition: To make accessible to customers or clients.
definitions.example: I will open the shop an hour early tomorrow.

definitions.definition: To start (a campaign).
definitions.example: Vermont will open elk hunting season next week.

definitions.definition: To become open.
definitions.example: The door opened all by itself.

definitions.definition: To begin conducting business.
definitions.example: The shop opens at 9:00.

definitions.definition: To begin a side's innings as one of the first two batsmen.
To bet before any other player has in a particular betting round in a game of poker.
definitions.example: After the first two players fold, Julie opens for $5.

definitions.definition: To reveal one's hand.
definitions.example: Jeff opens his hand revealing a straight flush.

definitions.definition: (of a file, document, etc.) To load into memory for viewing or editing.
To disclose; to reveal; to interpret; to explain.
An act or instance of making or becoming open.
definitions.example: He remembered fondly the Christmas morning opening of presents.

definitions.definition: Something that is open.
definitions.example: A salamander darted out of an opening in the rocks.

definitions.definition: An act or instance of beginning.
definitions.example: Their opening of the concert with Brass in Pocket always fires up the crowd.

definitions.definition: Something that is a beginning.
A vacant position, especially in an array.
definitions.example: Are there likely to be any openings on the Supreme Court in the next four years?

definitions.definition: An opportunity, as in a competitive activity.
In mathematical morphology, the dilation of the erosion of a set.
Pertaining to the start or beginning of a series of events.
definitions.example: The opening theme of Beethoven's Fifth Symphony is, perhaps, the most recognizable in all of European art music.

definitions.definition: Describing the first period of play, usually up to the fall of the first wicket; describing a batsman who opens the innings or a bowler who opens the attack
A small fruit, usually red, black or yellow, with a smooth hard seed and a short hard stem.
Prunus subg. Cerasus, trees or shrubs that bear cherries.
The wood of a cherry tree.
(color) Cherry red.
Virginity, especially female virginity as embodied by a hymen.
A subtree consisting of a node with exactly two leaves.
A cricket ball.
Cherrytop.
Burning tip of a cigarette.
Containing or having the taste of cherries.
Of a bright red colour; cherry red.
(often of cars) In excellent condition; mint condition.
Hard to see through, as a fog or mist.
Dark, dim, gloomy.
Cloudy, indistinct, obscure.
Dishonest, shady.
A gathering of people for the purchase and sale of merchandise at a set time, often periodic.
definitions.example: The privilege to hold a weekly market was invaluable for any feudal era burgh.

definitions.definition: City square or other fairly spacious site where traders set up stalls and buyers browse the merchandise.
A grocery store
definitions.example: Stop by the market on your way home and pick up some milk

definitions.definition: A group of potential customers for one's product.
definitions.example: We believe that the market for the new widget is the older homeowner.

definitions.definition: A geographical area where a certain commercial demand exists.
definitions.example: Foreign markets were lost as our currency rose versus their valuta.

definitions.definition: A formally organized, sometimes monopolistic, system of trading in specified goods or effects.
definitions.example: The stock market ceased to be monopolized by the paper-shuffling national stock exchanges with the advent of Internet markets.

definitions.definition: The sum total traded in a process of individuals trading for certain commodities.
The price for which a thing is sold in a market; hence, value; worth.
To make (products or services) available for sale and promote them.
definitions.example: We plan to market an ecology model by next quarter.

definitions.definition: To sell
definitions.example: We marketed more this quarter already than all last year!

definitions.definition: To deal in a market; to buy or sell; to make bargains for provisions or goods.
Freedom from some business or activity.
Free time given over to a specific purpose; occupation, activity.
A period during which official activity or business is formally suspended; an official holiday from university, law courts etc.
A holiday; a stretch of leisure time away from work or duty and devoted to rest or pleasure.
The act of vacating something; moving out.
definitions.example: The Conservative Party’s vacation of the centre ground gave an opportunity to its opponents.

definitions.definition: The act of making legally void.
To spend or take a vacation.
definitions.example: This year, we’re vacationing in Mexico.

definitions.definition: An instance of having won a competition or battle or succeeded in an effort.
definitions.example: It was a great victory on the battlefield.

definitions.definition: The condition of having succeeded in a conflict or other effort.
A winged figure representing victory, common in Roman official iconography. See Winged victory.
To achieve a victory
A glass-paneled cabinet or case, especially for displaying articles such as china, objets d'art, or fine merchandise.
An abstract or a condensed presentation of the substance of a body of material.
A deliberate action aimed at weakening an enemy through subversion, obstruction, disruption, and/or destruction.

An act or acts with intent to injure, interfere with, or obstruct the national defense of a country by willfully injuring or destroying, or attempting to injure or destroy, any national defense or war materiel, premises, or utilities, to include human and natural resources.
To deliberately destroy or damage something in order to prevent it from being successful.
definitions.example: The railway line had been sabotaged by enemy commandos.

definitions.definition: A New Zealander.
A flightless bird of the genus Apteryx native to New Zealand.
A New Zealand dollar.
A kiwi fruit.
A green-yellow colour, like that of kiwi flesh (also called kiwi green).
A member of the air force who does not fly.
To be, or cause to be, reborn, especially in a different body or as a different species.
To build with bricks.
To make into bricks.
To hit someone or something with a brick.
To make an electronic device nonfunctional and usually beyond repair, essentially making it no more useful than a brick.
definitions.example: My VCR was bricked during the lightning storm.

definitions.definition: A berserk (a crazed Norse warrior who fought in a frenzy).
One who fights as if frenzied, like a berserker.
A type of von Neumann probe whose mission is to exterminate alien lifeforms.
The ancient search for a universal panacea, and of the philosopher's stone, that eventually developed into chemistry.
The causing of any sort of mysterious sudden transmutation.
Any elaborate transformation process or algorithm.
To lay or place; to put (one thing to another)
definitions.example: to apply cream to a rash

definitions.definition: To put to use; to use or employ for a particular purpose, or in a particular case
definitions.example: to apply funds to the repayment of a debt

definitions.definition: To make use of, declare, or pronounce, as suitable, fitting, or relative
definitions.example: We need to apply the skills we've learned to solve this problem

definitions.definition: To put closely; to join; to engage and employ diligently, or with attention
To to address; to refer; generally used reflexively.
To submit oneself as a candidate (with the adposition "to" designating the recipient of the submission, and the adposition "for" designating the position).
definitions.example: I recently applied to the tavern for a job as a bartender.

definitions.definition: To pertain or be relevant to a specified individual or group.
definitions.example: That rule only applies to foreigners.

definitions.definition: To busy; to keep at work; to ply.
To visit.
A serious or devastating setback
An evil spell; a curse or hex
The vibrato system of an electric guitar, or just its lever (whammy bar)
A region where war is ongoing.
A region where structures are damaged or in disarray, appearing similar to the effects of a military conflict.
By extension, any area with a significant display of damage or disarray.
A custom or practice of a society or community.
An organization similarly long established and respected, particularly one involved with education, public service, or charity work.
The building or buildings which house such an organization.
definitions.example: He's been in an institution since the crash.

definitions.definition: Other places or businesses similarly long established and respected.
definitions.example: Over time, the local pub has become something of an institution.

definitions.definition: A person similarly long established in a place, position, or field.
The act of instituting something.
definitions.example: The institution of higher speed limits was a popular move but increased the severity of crashes.

definitions.definition: The act by which a bishop commits a cure of souls to a priest.
That which institutes or instructs, particularly a textbook or system of elements or rules.
A sweet mixture of fruit boiled with sugar and allowed to congeal. Often spread on bread or toast or used in jam tarts.
A difficult situation.
Blockage, congestion.
definitions.example: A traffic jam caused us to miss the game's first period.

definitions.definition: An informal, impromptu performance or rehearsal.
(by extension) A song; a track.
(by extension) An informal event where people brainstorm and collaborate on projects.
To get something stuck in a confined space.
definitions.example: Her poor little baby toe got jammed in the door.

definitions.definition: To brusquely force something into a space; cram, squeeze.
definitions.example: The rush-hour train was jammed with commuters.

definitions.definition: To cause congestion or blockage. Often used with "up"
definitions.example: A single accident can jam the roads for hours.

definitions.definition: To block or confuse a broadcast signal.
To throw a pitch at or near the batter's hands.
definitions.example: Jones was jammed by the pitch.

definitions.definition: To play music (especially improvisation as a group, or an informal unrehearsed session).
To injure a finger or toe by sudden compression of the digit's tip.
definitions.example: When he tripped on the step he jammed his toe.

definitions.definition: To attempt to score points.
definitions.example: Toughie jammed four times in the second period.

definitions.definition: To bring (a vessel) so close to the wind that half her upper sails are laid aback.
To give up on a date or some joint endeavour; stand up, chicken out, jam out.
Somewhat lop; inclined to lop.
A dish made of melted cheese, chocolate etc., or of a boiling liquid into which food can be dipped.
To prepare or serve as a fondue.
Italian pasta-like dumplings made of potato or semolina.
To sparkle with light; to shine with a brilliant and broken light or showy luster; to gleam.
definitions.example: a glittering sword

definitions.definition: To be showy, specious, or striking, and hence attractive.
definitions.example: the glittering scenes of a court

definitions.definition: The appearance of something that glitters.
Brightly sparkling.
Valuable, desirable.
Any plant.
A plant raised for some edible part of it, such as the leaves, roots, fruit or flowers, but excluding any plant considered to be a fruit, grain, herb, or spice in the culinary sense.
The edible part of such a plant.
A person whose brain (or, infrequently, body) has been damaged so that they cannot interact with the surrounding environment; a person in a persistent vegetative state.
Of or relating to plants.
Of or relating to vegetables.
A gathering at which people sing songs.
To produce musical or harmonious sounds with one’s voice.
definitions.example: "I really want to sing in the school choir," said Vera.

definitions.definition: To express audibly by means of a harmonious vocalization.
To soothe with singing.
definitions.example: to sing somebody to sleep

definitions.definition: To confess under interrogation.
To make a small, shrill sound.
definitions.example: The air sings in passing through a crevice.

definitions.definition: To relate in verse; to celebrate in poetry.
To display fine qualities; to stand out as excellent.
definitions.example: The sauce really makes this lamb sing.

definitions.definition: To be capable of being sung; to produce a certain effect by being sung.
A soft garment, usually knit or woven, worn on the foot and lower leg under shoes or other footwear.
definitions.example: a pair of stockings

definitions.definition: A broad ring of a different fur colour on the lower part of the leg of a quadruped.
A knitted hood of cotton thread which is eventually converted by a special process into an incandescent mantle for gas lighting.
Race; family; breed; kind.
Persons of the same race or family; kindred.
One or more relatives, such as siblings or cousins, taken collectively.
Relationship; same-bloodedness or affinity; near connection or alliance, as of those having common descent.
Kind; sort; manner; way.
Related by blood or marriage, akin. Generally used in "kin to".
definitions.example: It turns out my back-fence neighbor is kin to one of my co-workers.

definitions.definition: A place where races are held; a racetrack.
An easily-accessible conduit or tray for organizing runs of data or power cabling.
The canal for the current that drives a water wheel.
Unrelenting or unyielding in severity.
Unremitting, steady and persistent.
A gymnastic and recreational device consisting of a piece of taut, strong fabric stretched over a steel frame using many coiled springs as anchors.
Any of a variety of looping or jumping instructions in specific programming languages
To jump as if on a trampoline.
To rewrite (computer code) to use the looping or jumping instructions called trampolines.
definitions.example: trampolined code

definitions.definition: A domesticated plant, in species Cucurbita pepo, similar in growth pattern, foliage, flower, and fruit to the squash or melon.
The round yellow or orange fruit of this plant.
The color of the fruit of the pumpkin plant.
Any of a number of cultivars from the genus Cucurbita; known in the US as winter squash.
A term of endearment for someone small and cute.
The place where something happens.
definitions.example: Being near running water and good shade, the explorers decided it was a good locale for setting up camp.

definitions.definition: The set of settings related to the language and region in which a computer program executes. Examples are language, currency and time formats, character encoding etc.

A partially ordered set with the following additional axiomatic properties: any finite subset of it has a meet, any arbitrary subset of it has a join, and distributivity, which states that a binary meet distributes with respect to an arbitrary join. (Note: locales are just like frames except that the category of locales is opposite to the category of frames.)
An excessive and dangerous dose of a drug.
definitions.example: die of a heroin overdose.

definitions.definition: To dose excessively, to take an overdose.
definitions.example: Be careful not to overdose on those pills.

definitions.definition: To indulge in something excessively.
To dose to excess; to give an overdose, or too many doses, to.
: Of or pertaining to an electron or electrons.
Operating on the physical behavior of electrons, especially in semiconductors.
Generated by an electronic device.
definitions.example: electronic music

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to the Internet.
Any agent acting to eliminate, reduce, mask, or control odor.
An odor-controlling substance applied to the underarm to counteract odor from perspiration.
Acting or including an agent to eliminate, reduce, mask, or control odor
Slightly drunk or intoxicated; tipsy
Crooked, askew; awry
To have an absolute requirement for.
definitions.example: Living things need water to survive.

definitions.definition: To want strongly; to feel that one must have something.
definitions.example: After ten days of hiking, I needed a shower and a shave.

definitions.definition: (modal verb) To be obliged or required (to do something).
definitions.example: You need not go if you don't want to.

definitions.definition: To be required; to be necessary.
To be necessary (to someone).
A need.
A large medical facility, usually in a building with multiple floors, where seriously ill or injured patients are given extensive medical and/or surgical treatment.
A building founded for the long-term care of its residents, such as an almshouse. The residents may have no physical ailments, but simply need financial support.
A place of lodging.
Hospitable.
The mathematics of numbers (integers, rational numbers, real numbers, or complex numbers) under the operations of addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.
Of, relating to, or using arithmetic; arithmetical.
definitions.example: arithmetic geometry

definitions.definition: Of a progression, mean, etc, computed solely using addition.
definitions.example: arithmetic progression

definitions.definition: A ruminant mammal with antlers and hooves of the family Cervidae, or one of several similar animals from related families of the order Artiodactyla
(in particular) one of the smaller animals of this family, distinguished from a moose or elk
definitions.example: I wrecked my car after a deer ran across the road.

definitions.definition: The meat of such an animal; venison.
definitions.example: Oh, I've never had deer before.

definitions.definition: (except in the phrase "small deer") an animal, especially a quadrupedal mammal, as opposed to a bird, fish, etc.
(also dairy products or dairy produce) Products produced from milk.
A place, often on a farm, where milk is processed and turned into products such as butter and cheese.
A dairy farm.
A shop selling dairy products.
A corner store, superette or minimart.
(chiefly in the plural) A woman's breast.
Referring to products produced from milk.
Referring to the milk production and processing industries.
On food labelling, containing fats only from dairy sources (e.g. dairy ice cream).
The sky, specifically:
The abode of God or the gods, traditionally conceived as beyond the sky; especially:
The afterlife of the blessed dead, traditionally conceived as opposed to an afterlife of the wicked and unjust (compare hell); specifically:
(by extension) Any paradise; any blissful place or experience.
(by extension) A state of bliss; a peaceful ecstasy.
(with a modifier) Similarly blissful afterlives, places, or states for particular people, animals, or objects.
To transport to the abode of God, the gods, or the blessed.
To beatify, enchant, or please greatly.
To beautify, to make into a paradise.
(chiefly in the plural) An enemy.
Not friendly, appropriate to an enemy; showing the disposition of an enemy; showing ill will and malevolence, or a desire to thwart and injure
definitions.example: a hostile country

definitions.definition: Aggressive, antagonistic.
Unwilling
Of a hostile takeover.
definitions.example: Microsoft may go hostile in its bid for Yahoo as soon as Friday, according to a published report.

definitions.definition: A shelly concretion, usually rounded, and having a brilliant luster, with varying tints, found in the mantle, or between the mantle and shell, of certain bivalve mollusks, especially in the pearl oysters and river mussels, and sometimes in certain univalves. It is usually due to a secretion of shelly substance around some irritating foreign particle. Its substance is the same as nacre, or mother-of-pearl. Round lustrous pearls are used in jewellery.
Something precious.
A capsule of gelatin or similar substance containing liquid for e.g. medicinal application.
Nacre, or mother-of-pearl.
A whitish speck or film on the eye.
A fish allied to the turbot; the brill.
A light-colored tern.
One of the circle of tubercles which form the bur on a deer's antler.
The size of type between diamond and agate, standardized as 5-point.
A fringe or border.
A jewel or gem.
The clitoris.
(sometimes figurative) To set or adorn with pearls, or with mother-of-pearl.
To cause to resemble pearls in shape; to make into small round grains.
definitions.example: to pearl barley

definitions.definition: To cause to resemble pearls in lustre or iridescence.
To resemble pearl or pearls.
To hunt for pearls
definitions.example: to go pearling

definitions.definition: To dig the nose of one's surfboard into the water, often on takeoff.
A vocal sound; specifically, a purely vocal element of speech, unmodified except by resonance; a vowel or a diphthong; a tonic element; a tonic; distinguished from a subvocal, and a nonvocal
(Roman Catholic Church) A man who has a right to vote in certain elections.
The words of a song together with other sounds sung by a vocalist
Mischievous; tending to misbehave or act badly (especially of a child).
definitions.example: Some naughty boys at school hid the teacher's lesson notes.

definitions.definition: Sexually provocative; now in weakened sense, risqué, cheeky.
definitions.example: I bought some naughty lingerie for my honeymoon.

definitions.definition: Evil, wicked, morally reprehensible.
Bad, worthless, substandard.
A hybrid martial art derived from Muay Thai, karate and especially boxing during the 1960s to 1970s; more generally, any stand-up combat sport that combines kicks and punches.
definitions.example: The Japanese kickboxing gym, recently opened in the Thai capital, had been a great success (Black Belt Magazine, March 1973, p. 13)

definitions.definition: A person who is anti-fun, or prevents others from having fun.
An eating establishment in which diners are served food, usually by waiters at their tables but sometimes (as in a fast food restaurant) at a counter.
The amount of rain that falls on a single occasion
The occurrence of liquid precipitation, the fall of rain.
To take bindings off.
To set free from a debt, contract or promise.
To disable some kind of connection in software, such as a key binding.
Not bound; not tied up.
definitions.example: The hostages' hands were left unbound.

definitions.definition: Without a binding.
definitions.example: an unbound book

definitions.definition: The practice or sport of shooting arrows with a bow.
A group of archers.
A person who trains another; a coach.
A person responsible for treating injuries sustained by players during matches; a physiotherapist.
A running shoe or sneaker.
A patch for a video game that applies cheats.
A piece of indoor equipment allowing a bicycle to be ridden while stationary.
To force a person to the ground with the weight of one's own body, usually by jumping on top or slamming one's weight into him or her.
To face or deal with, attempting to overcome or fight down.
definitions.example: The government's measures to tackle crime were insufficient.

definitions.definition: To attempt to take away a ball.
To bring a ball carrier to the ground.
To "hit on" or pursue a person that one is interested in.
Made of ropes tackled together.
A discrete division of something.
A sharp extremity.
One of the several different parts of the escutcheon.
A short piece of cordage used in reefing sails.
A string or lace used to tie together certain garments.
Lace worked by the needle.
definitions.example: point de Venise; Brussels point

definitions.definition: An item of private information; a hint; a tip; a pointer.
The attitude assumed by a pointer dog when he finds game.
definitions.example: The dog came to a point.

definitions.definition: The perpendicular rising of a hawk over the place where its prey has gone into cover.
The act of pointing, as of the foot downward in certain dance positions.
The gesture of extending the index finger in a direction in order to indicate something.
A vaccine point.
In various sports, a position of a certain player, or, by extension, the player occupying that position.
To extend the index finger in the direction of something in order to show where it is or to draw attention to it.
definitions.example: It's rude to point at other people.

definitions.definition: To draw attention to something or indicate a direction.
definitions.example: The arrow of a compass points north

definitions.definition: To face in a particular direction.
(sometimes figurative) To direct toward an object; to aim.
definitions.example: to point a gun at a wolf, or a cannon at a fort

definitions.definition: To give a point to; to sharpen; to cut, forge, grind, or file to an acute end.
definitions.example: to point a dart, a pencil, or (figuratively) a moral

definitions.definition: To indicate a probability of something.
To repair mortar.
To fill up and finish the joints of (a wall), by introducing additional cement or mortar, and bringing it to a smooth surface.
(stone-cutting) To cut, as a surface, with a pointed tool.
To direct or encourage (someone) in a particular direction.
definitions.example: If he asks for food, point him toward the refrigerator.

definitions.definition: To separate an integer from a decimal with a decimal point.
To mark with diacritics.
To supply with punctuation marks; to punctuate.
definitions.example: to point a composition

definitions.definition: To direct the central processing unit to seek information at a certain location in memory.
To direct requests sent to a domain name to the IP address corresponding to that domain name.
To sail close to the wind.
definitions.example: Bear off a little, we're pointing.

definitions.definition: To indicate the presence of game by a fixed and steady look, as certain hunting dogs do.
(of an abscess) To approximate to the surface; to head.
To give point to (something said or done); to give particular prominence or force to.
To appoint.
Movable rails which can be used to switch a train from one railway track to another.

The two metal surfaces in a distributor which close or open to allow current to flow or not through the ignition coil. Each surface is called a point singular (there's usually a moving point which is pushed by the distributor cam and a fixed point which isn't), but they're made together in a unit and serviced or replaced that way and are hence normally called points plural.
An act of dodging.
A trick, evasion or wile.
A line of work.
To avoid (something) by moving suddenly out of the way.
definitions.example: He dodged traffic crossing the street.

definitions.definition: To avoid; to sidestep.
definitions.example: The politician dodged the question with a meaningless reply.

definitions.definition: To go hither and thither.
(videography) To decrease the exposure for certain areas of an image in order to make them darker (compare burn).
To follow by dodging, or suddenly shifting from place to place.
To trick somebody.
Dodgy
A bad habit.
definitions.example: Gluttony is a vice, not a virtue.

definitions.definition: Any of various crimes related (depending on jurisdiction) to prostitution, pornography, gambling, alcohol, or drugs.
A defect in the temper or behaviour of a horse, such as to make the animal dangerous, to injure its health, or to diminish its usefulness.
A mechanical screw apparatus used for clamping or holding (also spelled vise).
A tool for drawing lead into cames, or flat grooved rods, for casements.
A grip or grasp.
A winding or spiral staircase.
One who acts in place of a superior.
The act of varying; a partial change in the form, position, state, or qualities of a thing.
A related but distinct thing.
definitions.example: All of his soups are variations on a single recipe.

definitions.definition: The angular difference at the vessel between the direction of true north and magnetic north.
A line of play that differs from the original.
A technique where material is repeated with alterations to the melody, harmony, rhythm, timbre, texture, counterpoint or orchestration; but with some invariant characteristic, e.g. a ground bass.
The modification of a hereditary trait.
Deviation from the mean orbit of a heavenly body.
An ultraroyalist in France.
An extremist, especially an ultranationalist.
An especially devoted football fan, typically associated with the intimidating use of extremist slogans, pyrotechnics and sometimes hooligan violence.
An ultramarathon.
An ultra-prominent peak.
(usually capitalised) Code name used by British codebreakers during World War 2 for decrypted information gained from the enemy.
Extreme; far beyond the norm; fanatical; uncompromising.
definitions.example: an ultra reformer; ultra measures

definitions.definition: The result of no longer possessing an object, a function, or a characteristic due to external causes or misplacement.
definitions.example: In other areas, glacier loss creates serious risk of a dry period across the Third Pole, Wang said.

definitions.definition: The destruction or ruin of an object.
Something that has been destroyed or ruined.
definitions.example: It was a terrible crash; both cars were total losses.

definitions.definition: Defeat; an instance of being defeated.
definitions.example: The match ended in their first loss of the season.

definitions.definition: The death of a person or animal.
definitions.example: The battle was won, but losses were great.

definitions.definition: The condition of grief caused by losing someone or something, especially someone who has died.
definitions.example: Her daughter's sense of loss eventually led to depression.

definitions.definition: (financial) The sum an entity loses on balance.
definitions.example: The sum of expenditures and taxes minus total income is a loss, when this difference is positive.

definitions.definition: Electricity of kinetic power expended without doing useful work.
definitions.example: The inefficiency of many old-fashioned power plants exceeds 60% loss before the subsequent losses during transport over the grid.

definitions.definition: An exposed plumbing fitting; a tap or spigot; a regulator for controlling the flow of a liquid from a reservoir.
(game development) One or several systems that inject currency into the game's economy, thus controlling or preventing inflation
The act or process of learning secret information through clandestine means.
Chromium, when used to plate other metals.
The basic structural elements used in a graphical user interface, such as window frames and scroll bars, as opposed to the content.
Handguns (collectively)
To plate with chrome.
To treat with a solution of potassium bichromate, as in dyeing.
A decorative Native American object in the form of a hoop and net with attachments such as feathers, traditionally believed by the Ojibwa to “filter out” bad dreams.
A domestic cat breed with short legs.
The empty space in the center of a donut.
A small ball-shaped pastry, made in the same manner as a donut, roughly the size of the hole in a donut.
definitions.example: These donut holes are delicious!

definitions.definition: (welfare) An income bracket where one is without benefits, even though benefits are available in the next lower and next higher brackets.
A section of water for which no one has fishing rights that is completely surrounded by regions where various parties hold fishing rights.
A child.
A person of very short stature.
A player who mainly concentrates on increasing their character's power and capabilities.
A bitter, destructive feud, normally between two families, clans or factions, in which each injury or slaying is revenged: a blood feud.
(often preceded by personal) A motivational grudge against a person or faction, which may or may not be reciprocated; the state of having it in for someone.
A response.
A versicle or short anthem chanted at intervals during the reading of a lection.
A half-pillar, pilaster, or any corresponding device engaged in a wall to receive the impost of an arch.
To say something in return; to answer; to reply.
definitions.example: to respond to a question or an argument

definitions.definition: To act in return; to carry out an action or in return to a force or stimulus; to do something in response.
To correspond with; to suit.
To satisfy; to answer.
definitions.example: The prisoner was held to respond the judgment of the court.

definitions.definition: To be liable for payment.
One from whom a person is descended, whether on the father's or mother's side, at any distance of time; a progenitor; a forefather.
An earlier type; a progenitor
definitions.example: This fossil animal is regarded as the ancestor of the horse.

definitions.definition: One from whom an estate has descended;—the correlative of heir.
One who had the same role or function in former times.
A word or phrase which serves as the origin of a term in another language.
To be an ancestor of.
An inverted U shape.
An arch-shaped arrangement of trapezoidal stones, designed to redistribute downward force outward.
An architectural element having the shape of an arch
Any place covered by an arch; an archway.
definitions.example: to pass into the arch of a bridge

definitions.definition: An arc; a part of a curve.
A natural arch-shaped opening in a rock mass.
Curved part of the bottom of a foot.
To form into an arch shape
definitions.example: The cat arched its back

definitions.definition: To cover with an arch or arches.
One of the bones, usually bearing teeth, which form the framework of the mouth.
The part of the face below the mouth.
definitions.example: His jaw dropped in amazement.

definitions.definition: Anything resembling the jaw of an animal in form or action; especially plural, the mouth or way of entrance.
definitions.example: the jaws of a pass; the jaws of darkness; the jaws of death.

definitions.definition: A notch or opening.
A notched or forked part, adapted for holding an object in place.
definitions.example: the jaw of a railway-car pedestal.

definitions.definition: One of a pair of opposing parts which are movable towards or from each other, for grasping or crushing anything between them.
definitions.example: the jaws of a vise; the jaws of a stone-crushing machine.

definitions.definition: The inner end of a boom or gaff, hollowed in a half circle so as to move freely on a mast.
Impudent or abusive talk.
Axle guard.
The curved part of the cushion marking the entry to the pocket.
To assail or abuse by scolding.
To scold; to clamor.
To talk; to converse.
(of a ball) To stick in the jaws of a pocket.
Containing the maximum possible amount that can fit in the space available.
definitions.example: The jugs were full to the point of overflowing.

definitions.definition: Complete; with nothing omitted.
definitions.example: Our book gives full treatment to the subject of angling.

definitions.definition: Total, entire.
definitions.example: She had tattoos the full length of her arms.   He was prosecuted to the full extent of the law.

definitions.definition: Having eaten to satisfaction, having a "full" stomach; replete.
definitions.example: "I'm full," he said, pushing back from the table.

definitions.definition: (with of) Replete, abounding with.
definitions.example: I prefer my pizzas full of toppings.

definitions.definition: (of physical features) Plump, round.
definitions.example: full lips; a full face; a full figure

definitions.definition: Of a garment, of a size that is ample, wide, or having ample folds or pleats to be comfortable.
definitions.example: a full pleated skirt;   She needed her full clothing during her pregnancy.

definitions.definition: Having depth and body; rich.
definitions.example: a full singing voice

definitions.definition: Having the mind filled with ideas; stocked with knowledge; stored with information.
Having the attention, thoughts, etc., absorbed in any matter, and the feelings more or less excited by it.
definitions.example: She's full of her latest project.

definitions.definition: Filled with emotions.
Impregnated; made pregnant.
(postnominal) Said of the three cards of the same rank in a full house.
definitions.example: I'll beat him with my kings full! = three kings and two unspecified cards of the same rank.

definitions.definition: Drunk, intoxicated.
Fully; quite; very; thoroughly; completely; exactly; entirely.
To break apart.
To cause to be broken into pieces.
To break up and disperse (a file) into non-contiguous areas of a disk.
Broken into fragments
Composed of fragments
To evoke, educe (emotions, feelings, responses, etc.); to generate, obtain, or provoke as a response or answer.
To draw out, bring out, bring forth (something latent); to obtain information from someone or something.
definitions.example: Did you elicit a response?

definitions.definition: To use logic to arrive at truth; to derive by reason
An insane person, especially one who suffers from a mania.
A fanatic, a person with an obsession.
The machines constituting a production apparatus, in a plant etc., collectively.
The working parts of a machine as a group.
The collective parts of something which allow it to function.
The literary devices used in a work, notably for dramatic effect
A veterinarian or veterinary surgeon.
To make better from a disease, wound, etc.; to revive or cure.
definitions.example: This bandage will heal your cut.

definitions.definition: To become better or healthy again.
definitions.example: Bandages allow cuts to heal.

definitions.definition: To reconcile, as a breach or difference; to make whole; to free from guilt.
definitions.example: to heal dissensions

definitions.definition: To hide, conceal, and keep secret, especially for a secret society (such as the masons).
(now especially in the phrase "hele in") To cover or conceal (a seedling, plant, roots, etc).
(especially Britain) A group of sporting activities including track and field, road running, cross country and racewalking.
(especially US) Physical activities such as sports and games requiring stamina, fitness and skill.
(operations) The stock of an item on hand at a particular location or business.
definitions.example: Due to an undersized inventory at the Boston outlet, customers had to travel to Providence to find the item.

definitions.definition: (operations) A detailed list of all of the items on hand.
definitions.example: The inventory included several items that one wouldn't normally think to find at a cheese shop.

definitions.definition: (operations) The process of producing or updating such a list.
definitions.example: This month's inventory took nearly three days.

definitions.definition: A space containing the items available to a character for immediate use.
definitions.example: You can't get through the underground tunnel if there are more than three items in your inventory.

definitions.definition: (operations) To take stock of the resources or items on hand; to produce an inventory.
definitions.example: The main job of the night shift was to inventory the store, and restock when necessary.

definitions.definition: The genitalia.
Of, or relating to biological reproduction.
Of, or relating to the genitalia.
Of, or relating to psychosexual development during puberty.
A particular edible plant, Spinacia oleracea, or its leaves.
Any of numerous plants, or their leaves, which are used for greens in the same way Spinacia oleraceae is.
Plants with spinach-like leaves that are noxious in some way
Plants with spinach-like leaves that have medicinal use
